title: centaurea lusitanica
intro: Centaurea lusitanica, also known as Lusitanian cornflower or Lusitanian knapweed, is a perennial plant in the Asteraceae family, native to Portugal and Spain. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. Centaurea lusitanica has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Centaurea lusitanica can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat various health conditions, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
